author: zaixing-wang
title: Smart CI fails: fastcore 2.0.1 breaks ghapi 1.0.4 - L.starmap AttributeError

failed_jobs.name: Smart_CI
failed_jobs.symptom: AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap' in GhApi init

failed_jobs.name: ci/gha_overall_status_webassembly
failed_jobs.symptom: Downstream status check exits with code 1 due to Smart_CI failure

key_errors:
File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/ghapi/core.py", line 105, in __init__
    funcs_ = L(funcs).starmap(_GhVerb, client=self, kwargs=kwargs)
AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap'

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ".github/actions/smart-ci/smart_ci.py", line 190, in main
    gh_api = GhApi(owner=owner, repo=repository, token=os.getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"))
  File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/fastcore/basics.py", line 578, in __getattr__
    if attr is not None: return getattr(attr,k)
AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap'
root_cause: requirements.txt for .github/actions/smart-ci pins ghapi==1.0.4 but only specifies fastcore>=1.5.4 as a transitive constraint. pip resolved fastcore to the latest release 2.0.1, which is a major version bump that introduced a breaking API change: the L() constructor now returns a plain Python list in some code paths, losing the fastcore.basics.L class method starmap(). ghapi 1.0.4 calls L(funcs).starmap() in GhApi.__init__, which fails when fastcore 2.0.1 returns a bare list. This is the 4th occurrence of the same failure today — the root fix is to pin fastcore to a compatible 1.x version (e.g., fastcore==1.5.29) in requirements.txt, or upgrade ghapi to a version that supports fastcore 2.x.
reproduction_steps: pip install ghapi==1.0.4 fastcore==2.0.1 && python3 -c "from ghapi.core import GhApi; GhApi(owner='x', repo='y', token='t')"
recommended_actions: Pin fastcore to a compatible version in .github/actions/smart-ci/requirements.txt, e.g. add 'fastcore==1.5.29' or 'fastcore<2.0.0'
Alternatively upgrade ghapi to a version that is compatible with fastcore 2.x (check ghapi changelog for 2.x support)
Re-queue PR #36781 after the requirements.txt fix is merged to master
prevention_strategies: Always pin transitive dependencies to specific versions or version ranges in requirements.txt files used by CI actions. Use dependabot or a lockfile to manage dependency updates.
historical_context: This is the 4th occurrence today (2026-07-09). Prior occurrences: run 29001747652 (PR #36504), run 29000703414 (PR #36773), run 29000379233 (PR #36780). All affected PRs are unrelated code changes — the failure is caused by a newly released fastcore 2.0.1 breaking ghapi 1.0.4 across all CI runs.
